description: A designed slogan graphic on a light blue background with a faint watermark of the U.S. Department of Homeland Security seal (text 'U.S. DEPARTMENT OF HOMELAND SECURITY' arced around the edges). At the top, large dark-navy text reads 'Break our laws,' in bold roman type with 'lose your privilege.' below in italic, forming the central message. The lower portion shows two pixelated/blurred specimen immigration documents: a sample 'UNITED STATES OF AMERICA PERMANENT RESIDENT' green card (surname 'SPECIMEN', given name 'TEST V', USCIS# 000-000-310, Category IR1, with Statue of Liberty and flag artwork) and a sample U.S. immigrant 'VISA' issued by the 'US CONSULATE GENERAL LONDON' (surname 'TRAVELER', Immigrant Visa, IV Case Number LND2004I6000201, Registration Number 00000473). The faces on both documents are deliberately obscured/scrambled. The propaganda technique is a conditional threat framing immigration status as a revocable 'privilege' rather than a right, warning green-card and visa holders that lawbreaking results in loss of status. Target audience is immigrants/lawful permanent residents and visa holders; intent is deterrence and projecting an enforcement-first posture under DHS Secretary Noem. This is the same recurring 'Break our laws, lose your privilege' template used across multiple USCIS posts.
notable_text: Break our laws, lose your privilege.; PERMANENT RESIDENT; VISA; UNITED STATES OF AMERICA
